meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Project Management for Students: Level Up Your AI Apps
content.seo_description: Stop building basic apps. Learn professional project management using Antigravity and Claude Code to scale your skills and build a standout portfolio.
content.answer_block: To break past basic app limitations, you must shift from ad-hoc coding to structured project management. By integrating Antigravity for architectural planning and Claude Code for rapid execution, you transform chaotic scripts into scalable, production-ready systems, effectively bridging the gap between student projects and professional-grade software development.
content.intro:
You’ve built the to-do list, the simple chatbot, and the basic weather app. But now you're staring at a blank screen, paralyzed by the complexity of 'real' software. The leap from tutorial-follower to engineer isn't about learning more syntax—it’s about mastering the architecture of your ideas.

Professional development isn't just writing code; it's managing it. By adopting a structured project management workflow, you gain the ability to decompose massive, intimidating features into repeatable, testable modules. Today, we bridge that gap, turning your development process into a high-velocity engine.

steps.title: Define the System Architecture
steps.description: Before writing a single line of code, document your data flow and dependencies to prevent scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Architect. Create a high-level technical specification document for a [Your Project Idea] including database schema, API endpoints, and core user flows.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --init --ask 'Generate a README.md and a project structure diagram based on my architecture plan.'

steps.title: Decompose Tasks into Atomic Units
steps.description: Break your project into small, manageable tickets to maintain momentum and ensure clear progress tracking.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Break down this project into 10 atomic tasks. Each task must have a clear objective, success criteria, and estimated complexity level.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --ask 'Create a tasks.json file listing the 10 atomic steps we just defined and prioritize them by dependency.'

steps.title: Implement Test-Driven Development
steps.description: Use testing as your safety net. It ensures that new features don't break your existing codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests for the core authentication module using Pytest/Jest, focusing on edge cases and error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --run 'npm test' or 'pytest' after generating the initial test file.

steps.title: Automate Documentation Generation
steps.description: Keep your documentation in sync with your code to ensure maintainability and professional standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the codebase and generate comprehensive JSDoc/Docstring documentation for all public methods and classes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --ask 'Update the documentation in the docs folder based on the latest changes in the src directory.'

steps.title: Review and Refactor
steps.description: Perform a final audit to clean up technical debt and optimize performance before deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a code review on this module. Identify performance bottlenecks and suggest refactoring patterns to improve readability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --ask 'Refactor the identified bottleneck in the main controller and verify it with existing tests.'

pro_tips: Treat your Git commit messages as documentation for your future self; use the Conventional Commits specification.
Never start a feature without writing its test failure first; this forces you to understand the requirements deeply.
Use Claude Code to generate 'ADRs' (Architecture Decision Records) for every major library choice you make.
Limit your work-in-progress (WIP) to one feature at a time to prevent context switching fatigue.
Automate your feedback loop by integrating a linter and formatter that runs on every save.
content.next_steps: Once you have completed your first managed project, push your code to a public repository with a high-quality README that explains the 'why' behind your architecture, not just the 'how'.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop building 'apps' and start building 'solutions'. The difference between a hobbyist and a developer is the process. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
